steps.title: Identify the time relationship
steps.content: The main clause says Faure's design increased the electricity the battery could hold — a past-tense action. The relative clause describes what Gaston Planté did to that battery fifteen years earlier. The blank must show an action completed before another past action, which requires the past perfect (had + past participle).

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Check each choice against the past-perfect requirement:
  • Choice A — is inventing: Present progressive; describes an action happening right now, not fifteen years before a past event. ✗
  • Choice B — will invent: Simple future; describes an action that has not yet occurred, contradicting the fifteen-years-earlier context. ✗
  • Choice C — invents: Simple present; describes a current or habitual action, not a completed past action. ✗
  • Choice D — had invented: Past perfect; correctly shows an action (Planté's invention) completed before the main clause's past action (Faure's improvement). ✓
D is correct.
